mentals.text: MIND. ► Emotional. ► He was joyous, enjoyed society, and was good-humored, [11] . [Curative reaction of the organism in a man of the opposite disposition. -Hahnemann.] ► Good-humor; he was joyful and conversational, and was happy in his condition (after thirteen hours), [11] . [Curative reaction of the organism in a man of the opposite disposition. -Hahnemann.] ► Earnest mood, quiet, busy with himself, speaks very little, [1] . ► Very lachrymose, [1] . ► Sadness, without ability to assign any reason, [5] . ► Sad; he fears the worst results from slight causes, and cannot quiet himself, [13] . ► Mind phlegmatic, depressed, sad, apathetic, indifferent to everything, without peevishness or without being weak, [5] . ► Her mind seemed dead and sad, though not with weeping, [1] . ► Morose; she frequently cries at nothing, [1] . [10.] ► She was full of grief all day; she grieved over her condition and wept; nothing in the world pleased her (after fifty hours), [1] . ► She will hear or know nothing from any one; she wraps up her face and weeps aloud without cause, [1] . ► Anxious and fearful, [1] . ► Great anxiety; he dreads the future, [8] . ► Violent internal anxiety, so that he could not remain in any place, but without complaints, [1] . ► Anxious thoughts and events in the past came to him as if the past were present before him, which caused anxiety and anxious perspiration, then it became black before his eyes; he did not know whether the images were real or illusory, then everything seemed different, and he lost all desire to live, [1] . ► (When walking rapidly, it seems as though some one were coming behind him, which causes anxiety and fear, and he is constantly obliged to look around), [1] . ►► *Very peevish (in the morning); he wishes to throw from him everything which he takes in his hand , [1] . ► Fretful and sad, [12] . ► Fretful and peevish all day; does not know himself on account of despondency, and is extremely depressed (after thirty-seven hours), [1] . [20.] ► Fretful and restless all day, found red nowhere, [11] . ► Quiet fretfulness; he is vexed at everything which even does not concern him, [8] . ► *Fretful and disinclined for mental work (after two hours), [5] . ► Fretful and lachrymose, [12] . ► Every word vexes her; she cries even if one only speaks to her, [1] . ► Quarrelsome mood and yet lively, [13] . ► (Hypochondriac mood; everything seems indifferent to him; he would rather die), [1] . ► Alternations of mood, at first joyous, then anxious, at last quiet and contented, [11] . [The first mood is a transient reaction of the organism from one of fear and depression; afterwards, the primary action of the drug is again noticed in the anxiety, after which the reaction of the organism is again seen, and the quiet contented mood remains. -Hahnemann.] ► Intellectual. ► Dulness of mind, that keeps him from every work, [1] . ► Even attractive things made no impression upon him, [1] . [30.] ► *Disinclination for earnest work , [5] . ► He is mentally exhausted, has no desire to think, is not inclined to thought, and is indifferent to his surroundings, [5] . ► On attempting to grasp an idea, it vanishes, [1] . ► He does not know whether the things which are presented to him are from out of his memory, are real, or whether he is only dreaming of them, from 5 to 7 P.M., [1] . ► Thoughts disappear whenever he attempts to think or speaks of any subject, and if any one interrupts him or changes to another subject, he, for the moment, forgets, or cannot at all collect his thoughts, [4] . ► The thoughts disappear (memory disturbed by fantasies); whenever he reflects upon anything numerous and confused subjects crowd upon him, so that he cannot collect his thoughts, and forgets entirely of what he was thinking, [4] . ► *Weakness of memory; a few minutes after reading anything, he can recollect it only dimly, and whenever he thinks of anything, the sense escapes him, and he is scarcely able to recall it after long reflection , [8] .

head.text: HEAD. ► Confusion and Vertigo. ► Confusion of the head, as in coryza, [13] . ► The head is very confused, in the morning, with constrictive pressure in the vertex (after four days), [3] . ► Vertigo; he runs against a door in walking, [1] . [40.] ► Vertigo while lying in bed, in the evening, as if everything were turning around, [1] . ► Vertigo on stooping and suddenly turning the head; everything turned about in a half circle (only once), [1] . ► Vertigo, as if stupefied, in the house, not in the open air, [1] . ► Whirling vertigo, especially while sitting, relieved by walking about (after one hour), [2] . ► Whirling in the forehead and dulness in the head (after five hours), [12] . ► Dizziness (after eight hours and a half), [5] . ► General Head. ► When bending the head forward, it falls forward almost involuntarily, while sitting (after ten hours), [3] . ► Dulness of the head, as if vertigo would occur, while standing and talking, lasting a long time, [7] . ► Dulness and heaviness of the head (after half an hour), [7] . ► The head is constantly dull and the spirits depressed, [10] . [50.] ► Dulness of the head only at intervals; at times the head was quite free and clear, [1] . ► Heaviness in the head (after seventy-two hours), [5] . ► Heaviness of the head and weakness of the cervical muscles; he is obliged to lean the head backward, or to one or the other side (after twelve hours), [7] . ► Heaviness lying upon the ethmoid bone in the head, above the root of the nose, like a compression on both sides, [3] . ► Heaviness of the head, relieved by resting it upon the hand (after one hour), [5] . ► Pressure in the brain, especially in the occiput, towards the bones of the skull, and a pressure as from too great accumulation of blood, in the evening, before going to bed, continuing after lying down (after thirty-nine hours), [7] . ► On motion, headache, as if the whole brain would fall out; even during rest, sensation as if the brain were pressed together, separated from the skull and lying loose therein, [1] . ► Headache alternately stupefying and boring, [1] . ► Aching, like a tingling, in the whole of the head (after five hours), [12] . ► Violent headache, as if the brain were torn to pieces, in the morning, immediately after washing, but it afterwards disappeared, with violent spasmodic yawning, [1] . [60.] ► *Headache, as if the brain were compressed (mostly in the forehead), with paroxysms of roaring in the ears, which ceases much sooner than the headache , [4] . ► Sticking headache all day (after seventeen days), [1] . ► Pain in the head, as if everything would come out at the forehead, on stooping (after five hours), [12] . ► Tearing and pulling from the head down through the cheeks into the teeth (after thirty-six hours), [12] . ► Tingling and sticking in the whole of the head, worse on stooping and when walking, in the evening, for several hours (after thirty-six hours), [12] . ► Forehead. ► *Dulness of the head, anteriorly in the middle of the forehead, in a small spot as large as the tip of the finger , like a stupefaction; on the street he does not know whether he is going to the right or left; he is obliged to be very careful, [1] . ► Headache pressing outward and pressing asunder in the left half of the forehead (after half an hour), [6] . ► *On shaking the head, there is a sensation in a small spot in the middle of the forehead as if there were something heavy like a ball of lead in the brain which would not loosen , [1] . ► Dull pinching headache in the forehead, with stitches in the temples, relieved by walking about, but returning on sitting and standing (after four hours), [13] . ► Drawing pressure in the forehead from time to time, [7] . [70.] ► Pressure above the right eye, behind the brow, as from something hard, [3] . ► *Pressive stupefying headache, especially in the forehead , worse on moving the head and on standing up, [11] . ► Violent tearing pressure through the left half of the brain, especially violent in the forehead, gradually increasing and gradually diminishing (after fifty-four hours), [6] . ► Hard pressure in the right side of the forehead , [8] . ► Dull, painful, at times sticking, pressure from within outward, at first in the whole of the forehead, afterwards only in the left frontal eminence, which disappears during rest, but returns more violent on motion (after four hours), [7] . ► Drawing, cutting tearing in the side of the forehead, [11] . ► Tearing in the forehead, in the evening, while sitting; on stooping, a sticking in it, relieved by walking, [1] . ► Pressive sticking and drawing pain in the left side of the forehead (after two hours), [11] . ► *A pressing-boring stitch, lasting for a minute, in the morning, from within outward, in the left half of the forehead, so violent that it awoke him twice (after twenty-two hours and a half), [6] . ► Burning stitches on the frontal bone externally, [3] . [80.] ► Sudden stitches in the upper part of the forehead, so that he started up, [3] . ► Temples. ► Hard pressure in the head, in the region of the right temporal bone and on the vertex , [3] . ► Pressive pain in the left temple internally and externally, as from pressing hard with the finger (after one hour and a half), [8] . ► Burning-pressive tearing in the right temple, close to the eye (after seven hours), [6] . ► Drawing-tearing sticking in the left temple, as if in the bone, pulsative and persistent (after forty hours); on the next day it returned at the same time, now in the left, now in the right temple, then in the left frontal eminence, less violent, continuing for several days, [7] . ► Stitches in the left temple, [1] . ► Sharp, burning, needlelike stitches in the left temple , [4] . ► Dull stitches in the right temple, internally and externally, as if the bones would be pressed out, aggravated by touch , [8] . ► Vertex and Parietals. ► Headache in the vertex like a constriction from all sides and pressure, [3] . ► Sharp pressure on the vertex at times, [3] . [90.] ► A boring stitch from within outward in the vertex (after fifty-six hours), [5] . ► Some large dull stitches extending from the skull into the brain in the vicinity of the crown, together with external soreness in the spot, especially when touched, [1] . ► Sensation of soreness in the right parietal bone only when touched; he is unable to lie on the right side in the night on account of the pain (after eighty hours), [4] . ► Burning-pressive pain in the left parietal bone, just above the ear, extending inward (after two hours and a half), [6] . ► Burning-sticking pain in the left parietal bone, [8] . ► Occiput. ► Feeling as if the occiput were compressed internally and externally , [8] . ► A heavy pressing-asunder pain in the occiput while walking in the open air (after half an hour), [6] . ► Rheumatic pressive drawing in the occiput, commencing at the articulation, on bending the head forward, [3] . ► Painful drawing on and beneath the occiput, persistent on every motion of the head (after ten minutes), [8] . ► Transient burning stitches in the occiput, on the first day extending from the right to the left side, on the following days from below upward, [2] . ► External Head. [100.] ► The hair of the head falls out profusely, [1] . ► The hair can be pulled from the head with little effort, without pain (after four hours), [5] . ► *An itching scurfy eruption on the scalp and also above and behind the ear , [1] . ► Tearing in the head externally and in the teeth, [1] . ► Painful drawing in several places in the head externally, aggravated by touch , [8] . ► *Itching on the scalp , [1] . ► *Great itching on the scalp, which was scurfy and moist , [1] . ► Itching, like needle stitches, on the scalp, and fine papular eruption anteriorly towards the forehead, [3] . ► *Fine burning needle stitches externally on the vertex , [3] . ► *Itching biting on the scalp, which was aggravated by rubbing, lasting several days , [7] . [110.] ► Biting itching over the whole occiput, which obliges scratching, but is rather aggravated than relieved thereby (after fourteen hours), [7] . ► *Biting itching on the upper part of the occiput, with sore pain, recurring in the same place about the same time, in the evening , [7] . EYE. ► Objective. ► The eyes lie excessively deep, with blue raised rings around them, as after great excesses, lasting four days , [12] . ► The right eye is much larger than usual (more widely opened), (after seventy-eight hours), [3] . ► Inflammation of the white of the eye, with pain , [1] . ► At night, dry matter collects upon the lashes and in the outer canthus; in the open air, the matter from the eyes also dries and causes tension, [1] . ► The eyes close at times, though he is not sleepy, [3] . ► Subjective. ► *Dryness of the eyes, lasting all day (after thirteen hours), [7] . ► *The eyes are dry, in the morning on waking , with pressure in them, so that she cannot open them without assistance, [1] . ► *The eyes are dry, in the evening, with pressure in them , [1] . [120.] ► Burning pressive sensation about the left eye (after four hours), [6] . ► Pressure in the eye; she was frequently obliged to wink, [1] . ► When reading, the eyes soon begin to ache (especially in the afternoon); a biting and burning, and then they discharge some biting drops; he is obliged to avoid the light, because it makes them pain sooner, [1] . ► The eyes feel as if full of sleep, [1] . ► Coarse stitches in the eyes on exerting them, [3] . ► Orbit. ► Pain in the upper wall of the right orbit, just behind the eye, pressing the eye downward, lasting a long time and frequently recurring (after ten days), [7] . ► Heavy pressure above the right orbit in the open air (after three hours and a half), [6] . ► Pressure above the right eye and a drawing upward, [3] . ► Lids. ► The inner canthi are agglutinated, in the morning, [1] . ► Dry matter constantly collects in the inner canthus, which he is frequently obliged to remove, during the day, [1] . [130.] ► Pain, more biting than itching, in the left inner canthus, [1] . ► A not disagreeable burning in the right outer canthus, extending rather far back behind the eye towards the ear, and recurring by paroxysms (after one hour and a half), [12] . ► A biting-smarting pain in the inner canthi , [1] . [In a man who had never had any trouble with his eyes. -Hahnemann.] ► A constrictive sensation in the upper lid, that forces out tears, [1] . ► *Pain as if a hard substance were lying beneath the left upper lid (after thirteen hours), [5] . ► *Pressure in the upper lid all day, worse on closing the eye , [1] . ► Hard pressure in the inner canthus of the right eye, [3] . ► Tearing pressure in the outer canthus and in the region of the lachrymal gland (after seventy-two hours), [3] . ► Violent sharp-cutting pain beneath the left upper lid (after seventy-five hours), [5] . ► A tensive stitch in the outer canthus of the right eye (after three hours and three-quarters), [5] . [140.] ► *Itching on the margin of the upper lid in the open air , recurring after two hours in the other eye, relieved by rubbing (after three-quarters of an hour), [10] . ►► *Itching of the margins of the lids (after two hours), [1] . ► Violent itching in the inner canthus, most severe in the open air; is obliged to rub it, [1] . ► Lachrymation. ► Biting water runs from the eyes, in the morning, [1] . ► Ball. ► Pressive pain in the upper part of the right eyeball (after three hours and a half), [6] . ► Sticking thrusts in the eyeball, as if it would burst (after one hour and a half), [3] . ► Pupil. ► Dilatation of the pupils, [1] ; (after twenty-six hours), [11] ; (first days), [12] . ► Pupils greatly dilated for many hours , [12] . ► Pupils contracted (after half, and one hour), [11] . ► The pupils are contracted (after half an hour), after which they are greatly dilated, [13] . ► Vision. [150.] ► Dimness of vision, as if the eyes were full of water, with itching and fine sticking in the inner canthus, compelling him to rub them, [1] . ► The eyes are dim and so hot that the eyeglasses are dim, [1] . ► Vision dim for near and distant objects (after ten hours), [7] . ► Illusion of vision; on rising from a seat he seems to himself much larger than natural, and everything beneath him seems too low down (after twenty-six hours), [3] . ► When reading, it seems as if small black lightnings passed between the letters, and then whole lines vanished, [1] . ► While looking in the open air, black lightnings passed at times in front of the eyes, like a kind of flickering, [1] . ► She saw a flame of fire before the eyes when in bed in a dark night, [1] . ► While looking, a white veil seems to extend over the object and renders it invisible, [1] . ► A halo is seen about the candlelight, in the evening, [1] . EAR. ► External. ► Pinching burning-pressive pain on the left concha posteriorly (after eight hours), [6] . ► Middle. [160.] ► Sensation of coldness streaming into the right meatus auditorius like a cool breath, lasting several hours, [12] . ► (Drawing pain in the ear), [1] . ► A pinching and twinging in the left ear, [1] . ► Dull but deep stitches, within the ear, first the left then the right, [1] . ► A dull painful stitch, deep in the right ear, in the evening (after forty-eight hours), [10] . ► A stitch in the left ear (after thirty-one hours), [5] . ► A tensive stitch in the left ear (after eight hours and a half, and thirty-six hours), [5] . ► Hearing. ► Ringing in the left ear (after four hours and a half), [10] . ► Ringing in one or the other ear, on moving the head, which disappears during rest (after two hours and three-quarters), [11] . ► At times slight reports in both ears, as if wind blew into them, without diminished hearing, [3] .

Head confused and embarrassed; dull feeling of head with inability to perform any mental labour. Whirling vertigo, sometimes in evening, in bed, or during day, when sitting or lying; > by walking; or by turning rapidly round on the heel. Fine, burning, needle-like stitches, externally on vertex. Hard, pressive pain in vertex. Headache in the morning on waking, as if brain were bruised. Stupefying, pressing headache, as if brain were compressed. (Stupefying headache that she had had for three days goes away at once. R. T. C.). Pain in l. side of head with inability to keep eyelids open (produced. R. T. C.). Stunning pain in the head, sometimes alternately with boring. Heaviness in head, esp. forehead, above root of nose (> by resting head upon hand). Pressing in forehead as from a very heavy lump (wedge of wood or plug) which will not be shaken off; in morning, from motion and from stooping; > when at rest, and when leaning head against something. Dulness in small spot in middle of forehead. Violent pressing boring stitches in l. half of forehead, from within outward, in morning. Drawing, tearing, or lancinating pressure in the head. Headache, as if forehead were about to split, on moving it, or on stooping. Burning in l. temple; internally and externally, as if bones would be pressed out, from touch. Compressive or expansive pains in head. Semilateral headache, as if a nail were driven into brain. Lancinating headache. Sensation as if brain were loose. Feeling as if occiput were hollow or empty, or as if brain were not large enough for the space. Feeling as if all back part of brain was wood and couldn't think. Feeling as if the occiput were compressed, internally and externally. Head becomes more pulled down (agg. in an old rheumatic. R. T. C.). Neuralgia of scalp. Rheumatic and drawing pains in exterior of head. Tingling itching, sometimes also gnawing, in scalp, with pain as of excoriation; the skin peels off, with itching and biting; in evening and from getting warm. Much itching dandriff on scalp. Moist, fetid scald-head, with violent itching. Humid, scalding-itching, fetid eruption on back part of head, sides of head, and behind ears; when scratching, the itching changes place, but makes it more humid. (Eczema of scalp and other parts. Scald-head; hair matted together, very stinking. R. T. C.). Falling off of hair.

Dull feeling in head, with inability to perform any mental labor. Sensation of a round ball in forehead, sitting firmly there even when shaking head. Pressive stupefying headache, especially in forehead, < on moving head and while standing. Sensation as if head would burst, especially in forehead and when stooping. Headache as if brain were compressed, mostly in forehead, with paroxysms of roaring in ears, which ceases much sooner than headache. Dulness of head, anteriorly in middle of forehead, in a small spot as large as tip of finger ; had to be very careful when walking in street, did not know whether he was going to the right or left. Hard pressure in right side of forehead. A pressing boring stitch, lasting for a minute, in morning, from within outward, in left half of forehead, so violent that it awoke him twice. Hard pressure in head in region of right temporal bone and on vertex. Sharp, burning, needlelike stitches in left temple. Dull stitches in right temple, internally and externally, as if bones would be pressed out, < by touch. Brain aches, as if torn to pieces, morning on rising from bed, < from motion, > from rest and warmth ; headache passes off with much yawning. Sharp, burning, needlelike stitches in left temple. Dull stitches in right temple, < from touch. Feeling in occiput as if hollow, or as if brain were not large enough for the space. Feeling as if occiput were compressed internally and externally. Neuralgia of scalp, in a literary man, who had taken mercury and was debilitated ; pain < by excitement, worry, mental work, pressure of hat, and change of air ; > by quiet, solitude, rest and a warm room ; pain dull and stupefying, producing a muddled feeling in brain, or as if a hard substance were pressing on skull ; all mental work was a trouble to him ; could not find right word, and ideas were slow in coming. Painful drawing in several places in head, externally ; < by touch. Itching on scalp which was scurfy and moist. Fine burning needle stitches externally on vertex. Itching biting on scalp ; < by rubbing ; lasting several days. Biting itching on upper part of occiput ; with sore pain ; recurring in same place about same time in evening. Humid, itching, fetid eruption on occiput, sides of head and behind ears ; scratching changes place of itching, but increases oozing. Chronic tinea capitis sicca ; whole hairy scalp covered with a dark scaly eruption, thickest at occiput, gradually thinning out till it reaches line of hair on forehead ; violent itching, < at occiput ; scratching causes bleeding and discharge of clear, non-glutinous fluid, which dries very quickly ; hair is rapidly disappearing ; accumulated scales at thickest part, about on a line with mastoid process, measure a quarter of an inch in thickness ; the head is generally washed with soap and water daily, detaching all scales and leaving scalp red and moist ; after washing there is a feeling of tension over whole scalp, very disagreeable, and particularly so around occiput, where it is actually painful ; marginal blepharitis and ectropion of left eye. Purulent scald-head on occiput, with sour stinking smell, pediculi capitis, and itching preventing sleep. Crusta lactea ; crusts from under which oozes a yellowish excoriating discharge ; small vesicles form where crusts have been removed, soon burst and discharge a yellowish corrosive fluid. Painful sensitiveness of scalp, skin peels off, with itching and smarting, < in evening and from getting warm. Hair falls off, mostly from occiput and around ears, with humid, fetid eruption, or dandruff on scalp. Pressing, stinging and tearing pains in bones and in periosteum on cranium ; swelling and suppuration of bones (caries), with putrid-smelling sweat, day and night ; < from motion and contact. Burning stinging on head, mostly on left temple ; < from heat of bed, at 3 P. M., and when lying on it.

nose.text: NOSE. [170.] ► Sneezing, with coryza, [1] . ► Frequent sneezing, without coryza (after two, and ten hours), [11] . ► Sudden violent coryza, with catarrhal voice, lasting a quarter of an hour, at 2 P.M., [1] . ► Violent coryza, without cough, [1] . ► Violent coryza; tickling in the nose and sneezing, with discharge at one time of profuse mild watery liquid, at another of thick mucus, afterwards of thick slimy substances, [12] . ► Violent fluent coryza; one nostril is stopped, the other is not, with frequent sneezing, lachrymation and cracked lips (after three, and four days), [10] . ► Coryza; at first discharge of only thick mucus, afterwards of thin water (after four days), [8] . ► (Stoppage of the nose, in the evening, so that she could get not air through it, made talking difficult), [1] . ► The nose is sore internally, with a scab deep internally, [1] . ► A sore pain on the cartilage in the septum of the left nostril, as if it would suppurate, when touched, [4] . [180.] ► Smarting pain in one nostril, as if it were very much ulcerated, [1] . ► Itching in the left nostril, which disappears on touch (after seventy-eight hours), [5] . >>>>> Copyright © Médi-T ® 2000 Staph.

Nose ulcerated, with scabs, deep in interior. Violent fluent coryza, with obstruction on one side of nose, frequent sneezing, and lachrymation. Sneezing without coryza. Coryza, with ulcerated nostrils. Coryza; at first discharge of only thick mucus, after of thin water. Obstruction of nasal fossæ, with nasal voice.

Scratching in choanæ. Ulceration of nostrils, with scabs deep in nose. Frequent sneezing, without coryza. Coryza ; at first discharge of thick mucus, afterward of thin water. Violent coryza, one nostril stuffed up, much sneezing and lachrymation ; nasal voice.

mouth.text: MOUTH. ► Teeth. ► *The teeth soon become black, she is obliged to clean them twice a day, and still black streaks remain across them , [1] . ► *A tooth that had for a long time been a little decayed rapidly became hollow, within eight days , [3] . [210.] ► *A piece scales off from the posterior surface of an incisor (after twenty-eight hours), [8] . ► *Toothache caused by drawing air into the mouth , [1] . ► Toothache on eating, the teeth are not firm but wabble about when touched; he cannot chew his food properly, for when chewing or pressing the teeth together, it seems as though the teeth were pressed deeper into the gum; therewith the gum is white (after fifty-six hours), [8] . ► *Pain in a hollow tooth, always after eating, a biting drawing (but in the incisors a pressure), which is excessively aggravated in the open air, even while the mouth is closed, which gradually ceased in the house, for several days (after five days), [3] . ► *The hollow teeth are sensitive to the slightest touch, and if after eating the slightest food remains in the cavities, there is a violent pain, extending into the roots of the teeth, and the gum about the teeth becomes painfully sore , [6] . ► *Any cold drink seems to penetrate the teeth as if they were hollow , [12] . ► A painful draft in the teeth, from time to time, followed by throbbing in the gum, [1] . ► The teeth begin to grumble, while chewing, [3] . ► *A piercing drawing in a tooth itself, that becomes hollow, and in the corresponding one on the other side, in the morning , [1] . ► Pressive-drawing toothache in the anterior teeth, as after Mercury, worse at night towards morning, [1] . [220.] ► Violent drawing toothache, with swelling of the cheek, pressive headache in the same side, and heat in the face, [1] . ► Drawing pain, only in a hollow tooth, in the morning (after seventy-two hours), [3] . ► Pressive-drawing toothache on the right side, caused by cold water, [3] . ► *Tearing toothache, immediately after eating and chewing, as also after drinking anything cold , which disappears within half an hour, but immediately returns on chewing; drink that is not cool or liquid food do not cause pain; the pain is not caused by motion, but if it exists it is aggravated thereby, especially by motion in the open air, [3] . ► *Tearing, first in the roots of a hollow tooth, then extending forward into the crown, only immediately after eating and chewing, greatly aggravated in the open air; together with a pressure in the upper part of the crown of the painful teeth, extending towards their roots; when touched with the finger the other teeth also begin to pain (after nine days), [3] . ► *Tearing in all the roots of the teeth , with a feeling as if on edge when biting on them (after forty hours), [13] . ► *Violent tearing in the roots of the teeth , with drawing in the muscles of the face, now in one, now in the other side, [9] . ► *Tickling sticking in the back teeth of the right lower jaw (after a quarter of an hour), [4] . ► Biting pain in the four lower front teeth, especially at night, [1] . ► Gum. ► Swelling of the gum, with heat in the cheek, [1] . [230.] ► The inner surface of the gum is painful and swollen, and is even painful on swallowing, [1] . ► *The gum bleeds when pressed upon and on cleaning the teeth, for many days , [1] . ► The gum becomes eaten away, [1] . ► The gum becomes pale and white, [1] . ► A pimple on the gum, not of itself painful, but only when pressed with anything hard (after seventeen days), [1] . ► A vesicle changing to an ulcer, on the inner side of the gum, full of sticking-drawing pain, [1] . ► The gum of the upper and lower right teeth is spasmodically and painfully contracted, so that he cannot separate the teeth on account of pain, [9] . ► The gum is painful when touched, [1] . ► Painful drawing in the gum of the right incisor and canine teeth and in their roots, which extends into the muscles of the lower jaw (after twenty-six hours), [8] . ► Painful drawing in the gum of the last back teeth and in their roots, [8] . [240.] ► *Tearing in the gum and roots of the lower back teeth, while eating (after seventy-two hours), [8] . ► Tongue. ► White-coated tongue (after twenty-seven hours), [11] ; (after forty-six hours), [12] . ► Dryness of the tongue, together with tough mucus in the posterior nares, which stops them, [3] . ► Sensation of dryness of the tongue; collection of sour water in the mouth, together with hard mucus stopping the choanæ, [3] . ► A sore pain on the anterior portion of the tongue, [1] . ► Stitches in the tip of the tongue, even when not touched, [1] . ► Sticking pain on the margin of the tongue, when pressed against the palate, as if a splinter were sticking into it, disappearing when eating, [1] . ► General Mouth. ► Constant accumulation of mucus, without a bad taste in the mouth , [8] . ► A blister in the mouth, [1] . ► Raw and scraped, but very moist palate, [12] . [250.] ► Pressive smarting on the palate posteriorly only, when not swallowing (after four, and five hours), [3] . ► Burning scratching in the palate, when swallowing and when not, [8] . ► Stitches in the palate when it is dry, in the evening (after twelve hours), [3] . ► Stitches in the palate, extending into the brain, [1] . ► Saliva. ► Salivation, [1] . ► The mouth is constantly full of water, as in great hunger, [12] . ► Accumulation of water in the mouth after eating, a kind of waterbrash, [3] . ► Accumulation of saliva in the mouth, [1] . ► Bloody saliva (immediately), [5] . ► Taste. ► A qualmish insipid taste in the mouth, though food has a good taste, [1] . [260.] ► A disgusting bitter taste to food (after forty-six hours), [12] . ► Disgusting bitter taste in the mouth, [12] . ► Watery taste in the mouth, though food has a natural taste, [1] . ► Bread has a sour taste, [1] . ► Food has no taste, though he has an appetite, [1] . ► Bread has a sourish taste, [3] . ► Smoking tobacco has a biting taste, [1] .

throat.text: THROAT. ► Dryness of the throat, especially in the evening before falling asleep; sticking in the throat, on swallowing, [1] . ► *Throat rough, as if painfully sore, when talking and swallowing , [1] . ► (Drinking beer causes a scraping, disagreeable taste in the throat), [1] . [270.] ► Painful drawing, extending from the hyoid bone deep into the neck beneath the lower jaw, aggravated by touching the side of the neck (after forty-eight hours), [8] . ► Scraping sensation in the fauces behind the nares, as if one had drawn snuff through, [5] . ► Swelling of the tonsils and submaxillary glands, [1] . ► Swelling of the submaxillary gland, which prevents swallowing, for four hours (after three hours), [13] . ► The submaxillary glands are painful as if swollen and bruised , [1] . ► The submaxillary glands are painful to touch, and also pain by themselves, [1] .

stomach.text: STOMACH. ► Appetite and Thirst. ► Excessively ravenous hunger, even when the stomach was full of food, and when he ate again the food was relished, [1] . ► He is attacked with a violent sensation of hunger, with accumulation of water, a few hours after a large nourishing meal, [10] . ► An apparent sensation of hunger in the stomach, as if it were hanging relaxed, yet without appetite, [1] . ► Great desire for milk, [1] . [280.] ► Thirstlessness; he drinks less than usual , [8] . ► Eructations. ► Eructation several times (after a quarter of an hour), [10] . ► Eructations tasting of the food, [12] . ► Eructations of tasteless liquid, after eating, [12] . ► Tasteless eructations; but neither air nor anything else comes up, [12] . ► A kind of eructation; a large amount of mucus comes into the mouth from the upper part of the throat (after half an hour), [1] . ► Scraping eructations that affect the larynx and cause cough (scraping heartburn), [1] . ► Frequent empty eructations, [12] . ► Hiccough. ► Frequent hiccough, associated with nausea and stupefaction of the head (after three-quarters of an hour), [11] . ► Frequent hiccough , while smoking tobacco, as usual, [11] . [290.] ► Much hiccough, half an hour after supper, [1] . ► Hiccough, always after eating, [1] . ► Heartburn. ► Heartburn, during his usual smoke, [1] . ► Nausea and Vomiting. ► Nausea, in the mouth and pharynx, caused by eating, as if he would vomit (after nine hours), [11] . ► Nausea, even to vomiting, every morning, [1] . ► Qualmishness, [1] . ► He is qualmishness and unsettled in his stomach, for three days, [1] . ► Qualmishness, immediately; water collects in the mouth, with short isolated eructations as from an emetic which would not act, [12] . ► Qualmish sensation, in the morning (after one hour), [3] . ► Stomach. ► Fulness in the pit of the stomach, and pressure and sticking in it, [1] . [300.] ► Pressure in the stomach as from a weight, in the morning in bed after waking, not relieved by any change of position (after six hours), [1] . ► Pressure like a soreness, above the pit of the stomach, with nausea, [4] . ► A pinching-pressive pain in the pit of the stomach, which only disappears on sitting and bending the body forward (after one hour), [6] . ► Tensive pain in the epigastric region (after thirteen hours), [12] . ► Burrowing pain in the stomach, [1] .

abdomen.text: ABDOMEN. ► Hypochondria. ► A constriction in the hypochondriac region, oppressing the chest and impeding respiration, lasting three days (after two days), [10] . ► A tension transversely through the upper abdomen, in the hypochondria (such as hypochondriac complain of), causing anxiety and dyspna, in the morning in bed while fasting, [1] . ► Umbilicus and Sides. ► Cutting in the umbilical region seeming to be external, in three paroxysms, in the evening in bed, [1] . ► A long-continued dull stitch in the region of the navel, aggravated by expiration and by pressure (after eight hours), [8] . ► Pinching stitches, sharp and rhythmical, to the left above the navel, [4] . [310.] ► Rumbling in the left side of the abdomen (after one hour), [7] . ► Drawing in the sides of the abdomen, extending downward, as if the menses would appear (after four days), [1] . ► Hard painful pressure in the right side beneath the umbilicus , [4] . ► Pinching stitch in the left side, in the abdominal viscera (after thirty-eight hours), [5] . ► General Abdomen. ► Loud rumbling in the abdomen, [1] . ► Rumbling and colic, for many days, [1] . ► Rumbling in the abdomen after dinner, sounding like forming and bursting bubbles, [10] . ► Violent rumbling and gurgling in the abdomen, without pain and without emission of flatus (after one hour and a half), [12] . ► Great emission of flatus, [5] . ► Hot flatus (after thirty-six hours), [4] . [320.] ► Emission of indescribably offensive flatus, [12] . ► Incarceration of flatus in the abdomen , the first eight hours, [1] . ► An excessive amount of offensive flatus, for many days, [10] . ► Much flatulence is caused , and much flatus of a bad odor is passed, for thirty-six hours, [1] . ► The abdomen feels compressed, causing tightness of breath, [1] . ► Griping pain, twisting about, here and there, in the whole abdomen (after two hours and a half), [8] . ► Griping transversely across the abdomen, and drawing in the sides, in the abdominal muscles, as if diarrha would occur, [3] . ► Griping in the upper abdomen, in the morning, as if diarrha would occur, though he could not go to stool, [3] . ► Griping in the intestines, with emission of flatus (after thirteen hours), [5] . ► Transient pressive pain beneath the last ribs, as from incarcerated flatus, [12] . [330.] ► Pressure, together with heaviness and tension, in the abdomen, [1] . ► A tensive painful pressure in the abdomen as if he had eaten too much, and then pressed upon the abdomen, with nausea, and accumulation of saliva in the mouth, [1] . ► Heaving sensation and intimations of diarrha, in the abdomen, [3] . ► Bruised pain in the abdomen (after forty-eight hours), [10] . ► Drawing pain transversely through the abdomen, [1] . ► Drawing pain in the abdomen, as from flatulence, [1] . ► Cutting colic in the morning before stool, [1] . ► Spasmodic cutting in the abdomen, with trembling of the knees; during the day, on the slightest motion, especially after urinating; in the evening cutting, even when not moving, which is relieved by bending up, [1] . ► Cutting in the intestines, especially always after eating and drinking, together with so great nausea that water collects in the mouth, with great weakness; after the pain there is great heat in the face, and the blood rushes to the head, even the veins on the hands swell up, [1] . ► Cutting in the abdomen, with violent urging to stool, followed by a very thin scanty discharge, with internal chilliness of the head; the evacuation was immediately followed by a kind of tenesmus, [4] . [340.] ► Cutting and digging in the upper and lower abdomen, with desire for stool, followed by a thin stool, though little was passed; after the evacuation, renewed desire and increased colic, though without evacuation in spite of every effort, a kind of tenesmus, which, together with the colic, was only relieved after he had risen from stool, [4] . ► Tensive stitch in the left abdominal muscles (after thirty-two hours), [5] . ► Violent drawing burning stitches, seeming to extend from the right abdominal ring, as if in the spermatic cord into right testicle (which, however, was not painful to touch), while sitting, standing, and walking, most violent while stooping (after thirty-three hours), [7] . ► A persistent stitchlike pain in the abdomen, beneath the right ribs, only when beginning to walk in the open air, [1] . ► Hypogastrium and Iliac Regions. ► Rumbling in the lower abdomen and drawing in the intestinal canal, [8] . ► Bruised pain above the hips, in the loins, then extending to the navel, noticed on stooping, but also painful on touch (after eighteen hours), [10] . ► Painless swollen inguinal glands, which are most noticed when walking and standing, lasting many days (after thirty-six hours), [1] . ► Dull stitch in the left groin, aggravated by pressure, but disappearing on inspiration and expiration (after eighty-four hours), [5] .

rectum.text:
RECTUM AND ANUS. ► Constant pressive pain in the rectum, while sitting, [5] . ► A pressing pain deep in the rectum, for three-quarters of an hour after a hard stool, [1] . [350.] ► *A smarting sore pain in the rectum, for a long time after a stool , [1] . ► Violent itching in the anus, with pimples, [1] . ► *Itching in the anus while sitting, when not at stool (after seven hours), [5] . ► After an evacuation of stool, another ineffectual urging follows without any stool, in the rectum, [1] . ► He was frequently obliged to go to stool, without colic; always very scanty and very hard fæces were passed, with pain in the anus as if it would split, [1] .
STOOL. ► Diarrha, in the morning, preceded by cutting pain and nausea; the last stool consisted only of mucus, [1] . [The following four symptoms seem to have been caused by too large a dose, which makes almost every drug act as a purge; for the primary action of this drug seems to be a pressive colic at stool, constipation, and either a very scanty, hard, or more seldom, a thin evacuation, which has also been noticed by other provers. Compare ss. 326, 327, 332, 368, 369, 370.] ► Diarrha, with colic, the last part slimy (after forty-two and eighty-four hours), [1] . ► A thin stool passes unconsciously, with a sensation as if flatus would pass (after two hours), [1] . ► Diarrha-like stool mixed with flatus (after three hours), [1] . ► Several usual thin stools, several days in succession, [1] . [360.] ► In the morning, immediately after a hard stool, a very thin, copious, yellow one, [12] . ► Stool soft (after forty-nine hours), [5] . ► Stool soft, but difficult to evacuate, on account of constriction in the anus, as from hæmorrhoids, [3] . ► Difficult stool; first the evacuation of hard fæces followed by softer, with great distress and straining, as if the rectum were constricted, with continued but ineffectual effort, followed by tenesmus, [13] . ► A natural hard stool passed with flatus intermixed, [1] . ► Hard scanty stool, with burning-cutting pain in the abdomen (after ten hours), [7] . ► Hard, scanty, thin stool passed, with pressive pain in the anus (after twenty-six hours), [7] . ► Stool hard (first day); none (second day); hard again (third day); natural (fourth day), [10] . ► Constipation, for several days (first days), [1] . ► Stools twelve hours later than usual, and passed in small pieces (after fourteen, and fifteen hours), [7] . [370.] ► The stool was delayed a long time, on account of lack of peristaltic action of the large intestine, [3] . >>>>> Copyright © Médi-T ® 2000 Staph.

respiratory.text: RESPIRATORY ORGANS. ► Sharp irritation to cough, in the larynx, but slight cough, immediately after eating (after four days), [3] . ► Constant inclination to hack on account of tenacious mucus in the larynx, which he cannot loosen by coughing, [4] . ► Voice. ► Voice very weak on account of weakness in the vocal organs, though in other respects he is active, [3] . ► Cough and Expectoration. ► Cough and coryza, for several weeks, [1] . ► Cough, caused by tickling irritation, only during the day, [1] . ► Violent cough after lying down, in the evening, and at noon, with tenacious expectoration, [1] . [420.] ► Cough, with yellow expectoration like husks, worse in the forenoon from 9 to 12, less early in the morning (after five days), [1] . ► Cough, with expectoration, always of 5 to 8 drops of blood, and always preceded by a scraping sensation in the chest, [1] . ► Cough, with expectoration of mucus, [1] . ► Sharp cough and accumulation of water in the mouth always soon after eating; it seems as though this water would violently force through the pharynx and causes a cutting in it (after twenty-six hours), [3] . ► Sharp cough, which threatens to tear open the larynx, as if caused by costal constriction of the trachea without special previous irritation (after four hours), [3] . ► Easy expectoration of much mucus by hacking, [10] . ► Respiration. ► Dyspna towards the end of coition , [1] .

VOICE AND LARYNX. TRACHEA AND BRONCHIA. [25] [Staph.]. Feeble voice from weakness of vocal organs, after anger. Hoarseness, with much tenacious mucus in larynx and chest. Nasal voice from stoppage of posterior nares. While talking she swallows continually. Rawness in larynx from talking. Sensation of constriction and pressure in throat pit after anger, < when swallowing. Dyspnœa : with constriction ; after seminal emissions ; toward end of coition. Shortness of breath from fulness and constriction in hypochondria and upper portion of abdomen. Cough : spasmodic, hollow, with expectoration of yellow, tough, purulent mucus at night ; only during day ; after vexation or indignation ; < after eating meat ; excited by cleaning teeth. Sputum loosened at night and generally swallowed. Croupy cough in Winter, alternating with sciatica in Summer ; cough drove him out of bed compelling him to sit up all night, could not breathe while lying down, sensation as if something were loose in pit of stomach, like loose flesh, that pulled every time he coughed ; cough excited by tobacco smoke.

chest.text: CHEST. ► Tightly adherent mucus lies in the chest for the first six or eight hours and several mornings; afterwards, and during the day, easy expectoration of mucus from the chest, [1] . ► The chest feels weak; something lies firmly adherent in the trachea, that compels hawking, [1] . ► Oppression, like a constriction, in the chest, causing slow and very difficult inspiration; expiration relieves, together with anxiety and uneasiness, worse while sitting, better while walking, lasting five hours (after six hours), [2] . [430.] ► An oppression upon the chest, an uneasy sensation in the afternoon that drives him from place to place, and does not permit him to remain in any place, [1] . ► Uneasiness in the chest, [1] . ► A pressure in the chest, and a heaviness in it, while sitting, relieved while walking, [1] . ► Attempts to eructate cause a pressure and sticking, extending into the chest, [1] . ► The chest was painful externally, on touch, [1] . ► Pain in the pectoral muscles, as if beaten, in the morning on moving in bed, and during the day on crossing the arms; she felt nothing on touching the parts, or on breathing, [1] . ► A dull sticking-pressive pain in the cartilages of the last ribs on stooping, they are also painfully sore on touch, [1] . ► Painful stitches in the chest, making expiration difficult, [11] . ► Sticking cutting in the costal cartilages of the left side; it seems as though one had been cut, associated with stitches, [8] . ► Dull stitches in both sides of the costal muscles, while sitting, worse on leaning backward, and persistent during inspiration and expiration (after half an hour), [5] . [440.] ► Sticking itching between the costal cartilages , [8] . ► Front and Sides. ► Constant pain in the middle of the sternum, as from a sore, worse on rising up and stretching out the body; also pain on touch, like a tension and pressure, so that at times it took away the breath, [1] . ► Pain behind the sternum, as if suppurating, when coughing, [1] . ► Pinching pressure below the sternum, just to the left of the ensiform cartilage, [4] . ► Sensation of soreness behind the sternum, [4] . ► Fine itching, sharp stitches, which provoke scratching in the upper part of the sternum, just beneath the pit of the throat , [4] . ► Pinching pressure beneath the right short ribs (after one hour and a quarter), [6] . ► Pressure in the left side of the chest, not affected by respiration, [1] . ► Sharp stitches, with intermissions for several seconds, and lasting longer than usual, in the region of the fourth costal cartilages of the right and left sides; they slowly penetrate from within outward, without affecting inspiration or expiration (after fourteen hours), [8] . ► Violent stitch in the right side of the chest while sitting, on bending the upper part of the body obliquely forward and to the right side, [7] . [450.] ► Sharp stitches, which begin in the most posterior portion of the right ribs and extend in a serpentine course to the cartilages, [8] . ► A dull stitch in the left side of the chest, recurring after a few minutes, [3] . ► Tensive stitches in the left side of the chest, while lying and on moving, worse during expiration than inspiration, most felt on ascending steps, and at last followed by a persistent stitch, which almost takes away the breath (after sixteen hours), [5] . ► A persistent dull boring stitch in the left side of the chest (after thirty-seven hours), [5] .

heart.text: HEART. ► He woke from the afternoon nap with most violent palpitation, [1] . ► A beating palpitation on slight motion, [1] . ► Palpitation while walking, and when listening to music, [1] .

back.text: NECK AND BACK. ► Neck. ► Stiffness of the nape of the neck, [1] . ► A pressure and tension in the muscles of the nape of the neck and left shoulder (after half an hour), [1] . ► A tense pain in the left side of the neck and occiput, only at night, which frequently wakes from sleep, and on account of which he can lie upon neither the right nor left side, [11] . [460.] ► Tensive pressure in the side of the neck, [3] . ► Rheumatic pain, like a drawing, in the nape of the neck and between the scapulæ, in the morning; she was unable to move the arms on rising from bed on account of pain, and could not turn the neck the whole forenoon, several mornings in succession, with weakness of the whole body, lasting till noon, [1] . ► Rheumatic painfulness, like a drawing pressure and stiffness, in the neck, at its union with the shoulder, on bending the neck forward, [3] . ► Pressive drawing on the right side of the neck, not affected by motion or touch (after thirty-two hours), [8] . ► A drawing pressive (rheumatic) pain in the side of the neck, on bending it forward, [3] . ► A pressing-down sensation in the nape of the neck, [3] . ► Paralytic drawing in the cervical joints, posteriorly in the spinous process of the first dorsal vertebra, [4] . ► Fine tearing in the muscles of the neck (after five minutes), [8] . ► Pain, as from a sticking with a knife, between the last cervical and first dorsal vertebræ, [3] . ► Jerklike stitches in the side of the neck, almost behind the ear, in the evening, [12] . [470.] ► A tensive stitch in the left cervical muscles, [5] . ► Itching in the nape of the neck, [1] . ► Back. ► (Pain, like a beating and jerking, in the back at night, lasting from evening till 5 A.M., so that it took away his breath, with slumbering), [1] . ► Violent stitches, extending up the back (after seven days), [1] . ► Dorsal. ► Burning pressive pain beneath the right scapula, close to the spine, with an acute sensation of heaviness in the right side of the chest (after two hours), [6] . ► Fine tearing in the head of the left scapula, aggravated by motion, [8] . ► A drawing pressure in the two first dorsal vertebræ, together with a smarting sensation (after one hour and three-quarters), [3] . ► Hard pressure in the left side of the dorsal muscles, near the spine (after four days), [8] . ► Lumbar. ► A pressure, as if beaten, in the small of the back all night; the pain awoke her when it was worse, about 4 A.M.; on rising up it disappeared, [1] . ► *Pain in the small of the back, causing less distress while walking than on rising from a seat, turning the body in bed, and on every motion sideways, lasting several days (after ten hours), [10] . [480.] ► A pain in the small of the back, extending downward, worse while stooping than when standing erect, least felt while sitting, [1] . ► *Pain in the small of the back, as if everything were broken to pieces, in the morning in bed; on rising from bed she could raise nothing from the ground till 8 or 9 o'clock, followed by hunger, afterwards colic and diarrha, which was at last slimy , [1] . ► *In the small of the back stitches and pain, as from a sprain, during rest, ceasing on walking , [1] . ► Sacral. ► Drawing stitches, at times jerking, in the sacrum, while sitting, [7] . ► A violent burning, externally, on the lower portion of the sacrum (after half an hour), [7] .

Drawing, rheumatic pressure, and tension in nape, with rigidity. Stiff-neck, shoulders sore to lie on (produced. R. T. C.). Weakness of muscles in nape and neck. Eruption of itching pimples on nape. Painful swelling of glands of neck, of nape, and under axillæ. Lancinations under axillæ. Pain, as if broken in loins, or sensation as from overlifting, or straining the back, esp. during repose, and principally night and morning. Pain in loins on rising from a seat, or on turning in bed. Itching stitches in region of kidneys. Violent (stitches) lancinations, which pass up back. (Suppurating swelling in the psoas muscle.). Lumbar abscess.

Painful swelling of glands of throat, neck and axillæ. Itching pimples on nape of neck. Rheumatic drawing, pressure and tension in neck, with stiffness. Pain as from a sprain in right shoulder, only in motion. Violent pressive pain in left shoulder joint, not > by any motion. Dull sticking pains in shoulder joint, < on touch and motion. Itching stitches in both axillæ. Pain in small of back as if broken to pieces, in morning in bed. Pain in small of back as after overlifting ; < at rest, at night and in morning, and when rising from a seat. In small of back stitches and pain, as from a sprain, during rest, ceasing on walking. All joints are stiff in morning on rising from bed, especially shoulders, and sacral and hip joints. Violent stitches upward in back. Suppurating swelling in psoas muscles.

extremities.text: EXTREMITIES. ► *All the joints are stiff, in the morning on rising from bed, especially the shoulders, and lumbar and hip joints , [1] . ► *All the limbs are affected and everything is sore , the muscles on touch, the joints on motion, more in the forenoon than in the afternoon, [1] . ► *The limbs, with the shoulder and hip-joints , feel beaten and painful, as after a long walk, [3] . ► An internal trembling of the limbs if held long in one position, in the morning (after twenty-four hours), [3] . ► A drawing (?) pain in the joints of the shoulder, elbow, wrist, fingers, back, knees, on moving them, less during rest, especially in the evening, [12] . [490.] ► Cramp in the fingers, and in various parts of the limbs, [4] . ► Sharp, deeply-piercing stitches at long intervals, in various parts of the limbs (after a quarter of an hour), [4] .

Paralytic pressure in both upper and forearms, < by motion and touch. Herpes : with scabs on elbows ; on hands. Eruption of small pustules on arms and hands, gradually spreading, and exuding a yellowish corroding discharge ; severe tensive burning pains. Tetter on hands, itches in evening and burns after scratching. Numbness in tips of fingers. Sensation as if a hard skin were drawn over tips of fingers of left hand ; there is little feeling in them, and sensation of touch is suspended. Paralytic drawing pain in first joints of fingers at union of metacarpal bones, < by motion. Fine jerking tearing in muscles of several fingers and in thumb, especially in tips. Deep burning itching, sharp, needlelike stitches in left thumb, provokes scratching. Osteitis of phalanges of fingers. First phalanx of left index finger affected ; ulceration and swelling about joint. Bones, especially of fingers, imperfectly developed. Arthritic nodosities on fingers. Nates ache while sitting ; pain extends to small of back, sacrum and hip joint. Sticking itching in gluteal muscles. Pulsating in hip joint, as from beginning suppuration. Aching pain around hip joint when walking and sitting. Daily pains beginning at crest of ilium, right side, extending backward and downward to thigh, < early morning, on rising or on sitting down, > standing and from warmth. Exceedingly severe pain in right leg, extending into genitals, particularly testicles ; attacks followed by great prostration. θ Sciatica. Morbus coxarius, with abscess in right iliac region ; temperature 103.2°F, pulse 120 ; right leg one-half inch shorter than left ; right foot everted and gluteo-femoral crease on that side obliterated ; limps in walking ; night sweats ; had a severe fall before present series of symptoms. Crural neuralgia, stinging, stitching pains during movement. Mrs. A., æt. 30, never pregnant ; menses irregular, uncertain, always late, sometimes wanting, but profuse, continuing a week or longer, consisting at first of pale blood, which became dark and clotted ; occasionally spasmodic uterine contractions ; from ten days to two weeks previous to menses, sharp pains during motion, extending from point of egress of right crural nerve on thigh down over inner surface of knee, to great toe ; constant aching of limbs, with great heaviness during rest ; after first sense of pain, paralysis of leg, till catamenia fully appeared, when it gradually became normal, dull aching of nates while sitting, extending to sacral and hip joint regions and small of back ; dull stitches during movement, and drawing sensation during rest, about knee and patella ; paroxysms of stinging pain along fibula, near malleolus ; sweat at night, great general prostration ; sinking feeling in stomach and abdomen ; complete amelioration of all pains during rest at night. θ Sciatica. Aching in thighs, when walking. Fine itching stitches in inner side of thigh provoke scratching. Legs painfully weak, especially knees. Stitches in knee and knee joint. Drawing stitches in right knee joint, < by motion. Dull stitches in knee joint near patella ; on touch stitches change to pressive pain. Dull stitches in right knee joint, immediately after rising in morning, < by motion. Burning stitches on outer side beneath left knee, at intervals. Sensation of stiffness and contraction in hollows of knees ; dribbling of urine. Tearing pain in muscles of one or other leg, while standing and sitting. Burning stitch in right tibia during rest. Sticking tearing in and beneath right calf and above left heel. Sticking itching just above right outer malleolus ; provokes scratching but leaves no special sensation. On putting foot to ground, pricking in balls of feet as if toes would be drawn down. All the limbs are affected ; everything is sore. Osteitis of phalanges, with sweating and suppuration. Arthritic nodosities of joints, especially of fingers. Gout, rheumatic gout, and gouty nodosities, in corpulent men advanced in life, feeble pulse, palpitation dyspnœa on exertion, pains in smaller joints of hands and feet, with much swelling and hardness ; generally skin coaffection, alternating with pains in joints ; soles of feet tender, knees weak. Acute articular rheumatism of fast or debilitated men, with shifting pains.

upper_extremities.text: SUPERIOR EXTREMITIES. ► Weakness and heaviness in both arms, as if lead were in them, after a nocturnal emission, [1] . ► Pressive drawing, here and there, in the upper limbs, aggravated by touch (after seven hours), [8] . ► A pressive drawing in the right arm, in the evening in bed, [1] . ► Pain in the bones of the arm, neither during rest nor when touched, but only during motion, [1] . ► Shoulder. ► Pressive drawing in the shoulder-joints, in the morning in bed, and immediately after rising; worse on motion (after five days), [8] . ► Paralytic drawing in the shoulder-joint, at times also in the whole arm, when lying under the head in bed (after ninety hours), [4] . ► Pain as from a sprain, in the right shoulder, only on motion , [8] . ► Violent pressive pain in the left shoulder-joint, not relieved by any motion (after thirty-six hours), [6] . [500.] ► A pressing downward of the shoulder, as if a weight were lying upon it, while sitting, [3] . ► A dull pressive pain in the right axilla, [12] . ► A loose pressure upon the shoulder, which is painful to touch, as though the flesh were loose, while walking, [3] . ► A drawing sticking in the left shoulder-joint, especially on moving the arm toward the chest, [7] . ► A pressive stitch, from below upward, in the right shoulder (after four hours and a half), [5] . ► Dull sticking pains in the shoulder-joint, worse on touch and motion , [8] . ► Itching stitches in both axillæ (after five minutes), [4] . ► Itching needlelike stitches in the right axilla (after three minutes), [8] . ► Stitches in the left axilla, [5] . ► Arm. ► Paralytic pressure in both upper and forearms, aggravated by motion and touch (after five days), [8] . [510.] ► Pressive drawing in the deltoid muscle, [3] . ► Hard pressure on the right upper arm, extending inward, aggravated by touch (after two hours), [8] . ► Paralytic pressive pain in the left upper arm, aggravated by touch (after seventy-two hours), [8] . ► Paralytic pressive pain in the left upper arm, aggravated by touch and motion; the arm is weakened (after thirty-six hours), [8] . ► Pain in the right humerus, an intolerable pressure in the periosteum, during rest and motion; the place was still more painful when touched (after thirty-six hours), [1] . ► Tearing pain in the left upper arm, in the deltoid, while sitting, disappearing on motion, [3] . ► Tearing pain in the muscles of the left upper arm, close to the elbow, [11] . ► Stitchlike tearing in the muscles of the right upper arm near the elbow, [11] . ► Elbow. ► Paralytic weakness about the elbow (after two hours), [3] . ► A sensation near the bend in the elbow, more towards the forearm, as from an eruption, or as if it were irritated with a needle; a kind of grinding, somewhat burning; though nothing could be seen yet it was especially painful to touch, [12] . ► Forearm. [520.] ► A pressive drawing like a pinching, on the outer side of the radius beneath the left elbow, [4] . ► Pressive drawing in the muscles of the forearm and on the back of the hand, [3] . ► Drawing-tearing pain in the forearm, especially on moving the arm and hand, [1] . ► Sticking tearing in the left forearm (after one hour), [10] . ► Jerking in the left forearm, during rest (after seventy-five hours), [5] . ► Slow dull stitches, like pressure, in the middle of the forearm, [4] . ► Wrist. ► A drawing pressure transversely across the wrist, especially on motion, [3] . ► Cramplike pain about the right wrist, which disappears on stretching out the fingers, but returns on bending them up, and is then associated with a tearing stitch, which extends through the whole arm to the shoulder (after twenty-four hours and a half), [6] . ► Sticking tearing in the left wrist (after one hour), [10] . ► Hand. ► Drawing pains through the bones on the back of the hand, especially on motion, [3] . [530.] ► Painful drawing in the phalanges of the right hand (after five hours), [8] . ► Sharp tingling stitches in the palm of the hand (after one hour), [4] . ► Tickling itching in the palm of the left hand, provoking scratching, [11] . ► Fingers. ► When the fingers are outstretched free they have a convulsive movement up and down, [4] . ► Sensation as if a hard skin were drawn over the tips of the fingers of the left hand; there is little feeling in them, and the sensation of touch is suspended , [8] . ► Paralytic drawing pain in the first joints of the fingers at the union of the metacarpal bones, aggravated by motion , [8] . ► Painful drawing in the metacarpus of the right index finger, [8] . ► Hard pressure on the metacarpal bone of the left index finger, aggravated by touch and motion of the fingers (after four minutes), [8] . ► Intermittent pressive pain in the metacarpal bone of the left thumb, aggravated by touch, [8] . ► Persistent pressive pain in the metacarpal joint of the right middle finger, extending forward, persistent even during motion (after seventy-seven hours), [5] . [540.] ► Pressive cramplike pain in the ball of the right little finger, on moving the hand, [11] . ► Fine jerking tearing in the muscles of several fingers, especially in their tips , [8] . ► Fine jerking tearing in the muscles of the thumb, especially violent at the tip (after forty-five hours), [8] . ► Tearing pain in the muscles of the ball of the left thumb, which disappears on moving the thumb, [11] . ► Tensive stitches in the tip of the left thumb (after fifty-two hours), [5] . ► Pain like needle stitches, in the middle phalanx of the right index finger and in its articulation, persistent during motion (after fifty-four hours), [5] . ► Crawling in the fingers as if they would go to sleep (after four hours and a half), [7] . ► Deep burning-itching, sharp, needlelike stitches in the left thumb, which provokes scratching , [4] . >>>>> Copyright © Médi-T ® 2000 Staph.

generalities.text: GENERALITIES. ► He becomes pale, dizzy, and falls to the side as in a faint, in the forenoon, after rising from a seat; a similar attack occurs about the same time the following day, [1] . ► The whole body starts up, frequently for several nights, both the arms and legs, as if some one suddenly tickled them, a kind of spasmodic jerking, though painless; he was very hot though lightly covered, but without thirst and without sweat, [1] . ► *Weakness of the whole body, especially of the knees, when walking , [5] . ► *Weariness and weakness of the body, in the morning (after four hours and a half), [7] . ► *In the morning in bed she is very weary without sleepiness , all the limbs are sore as if bruised, and as if there were no strength in them, for an hour , [1] . ► Great weariness and inclination to sleep, in the afternoon while sitting (after three days), [7] . ► Great weariness, in the morning on waking, which, however, soon disappears, [1] . ► *Great weariness and sleepiness, after eating, feels the need of lying down , soon falls asleep, wakes confused and heavy in the limbs, and dreads to walk; on continuing to walk, which is very irksome, especially on ascending a hill, he feels more active and lively, indeed is more vigorous after a long ramble, [12] . ► *Painfulness of the whole body as if bruised, with an excessively weak sensation , worse on motion, renewed and aggravated by walking after sitting (after forty hours), [12] . [610.] ► *Pain in all the bones , [1] . ► *General bruised sensation when walking , better when sitting and lying down; especially in the calves; an excessively weary pain as if beaten; she could scarcely drag along the feet , [12] . ► Paralytic drawing in various parts of the body, especially in the joints, if the limbs are kept long in an unusual or uncomfortable position, [4] . ► *Drawing-tearing pain, here and there, in the muscles of the whole body, while sitting (after eight and a half, and thirty-four hours), [7] . ► Sharp itching stitches in various parts of the body , [4] .

Clinical: Adenoids; Anger, fits of; Anus, itching of; Backache; Bashfulness; Blepharitis; Bones, diseases of; Cauliflower excrescences; Chalazion; Condylomata; Cough; Cysts; Dentition; Dysentery; Dysparunia; in newly married women; Eczema; Eyes, tumours on; Fistula dentalis; Gastralgia; Glands, affections of; Hip-joint disease; Hypochondriasis; Impotency; Iritis; syphilitic; Jaw-joint, easy dislocation of; Lumbar abscess; Mania; Masturbation, effects of; Neuralgia; Night-sweats; Nymphomania; Ovaries, affections of; Pediculosis; Perspiration, offensive; Pregnancy, nausea of; Prostate, affections of; Psoas abscess; Ranula; Rheumatism; Sciatica; Scurvy; Sea-sickness; Seborrhœa; Self-abuse; Spermatic cords, affections of; Spermatorrhœa; Steatoma; Stiff-neck; Styes; Swallowing, constant while talking; Teeth, caries of; Testicles, affections of; Tibiæ, pains in; Tobacco, effect of; Toenail, ingrowing; Tonsillitis; Toothache; Tumours; tarsal; Voice, nasal; hoarse; Warts
Causation: Anger. Anger suppressed or reserved. Injury; falls; clean-cut wounds; operations. Coitus. Masturbation. Sexual abuse. Sexual craving. Emissions. Dentition. Tobacco. Mercury.

Hoarseness, with accumulation of mucus, which adheres to larynx and chest. Sensation of pressure and contraction in pit of throat, after a fit of passion, by deglutition. Roughness, rawness, in larynx, after much speaking. Dry, hollow cough, excited by a tickling in the larynx. Violent cough, with expectoration of viscid mucus, in evening, after lying down. Violent spasmodic cough, with (tough) purulent, yellow expectoration, esp. at night. Croupy cough in winter, alternating with sciatica in summer; cough excited by tobacco smoke. Expectoration of blood, when coughing. Dyspnœa: with constriction; after seminal emissions; towards end of coition. Pain (soreness and rawness) as from ulceration in the chest, during cough. Dyspnœa with constriction and restlessness in chest. Drawing, tearing, stitching pains in extremities. Limbs feel beaten and painful, as after a long walk, below shoulders and below hip-joint. Aching of shoulders. Stitches in shoulder-joints, on touch and motion. Dislocation pains in r. shoulder-joint, only on moving. Stiffness of shoulder-joint in morning. Shoulders sore to lie on, and stiff-neck (produced. R. T. C.). Pains in bones of arms, during movement. Pressive, paralytic, drawing, and lancinating tearings, in arms ( on motion and touch), and forearms, shoulders, hands, and fingers. Painful pressure in bone of arm. Paralytic drawing in metacarpal joints, from motion. Hands become anæmic from cardiac inertia; gouty pains in little finger, index and thumb of l. hand at night, and loss of power, in l. thumb with pain in r. shoulder (produced. R. T. C.). Herpes on hands. Herpes with scabs on elbows. Numbness in ends of fingers. Jerking tearing in fingers, esp. in the tips. Burning itching in l. thumb. Arthritic nodosities in joints of the fingers. Osteitis of phalanges of fingers. Cramps in fingers. Convulsive movements of fingers. Pulsating pain in hip-joint as from beginning suppuration. Stiffness of coxo-femoral joint in morning. Nates ache while sitting; pain extends to small of back, sacrum, and hip-joint. Daily pains beginning at crest of ilium, r. side, extending backward and downward to thigh, early morning, on rising or on sitting down, > standing and from warmth. Exceedingly severe pain in r. leg, extending into genitals, esp. testes; attacks followed by great prostration. Painful weakness of thighs and legs, esp. of knee-joint. Pain as of a fracture in thighs when walking. Itching tetters on thighs and legs. Itching on inner side of thighs. Drawing shootings in the knees and knee-joints ( on motion). Drawings, and pressive tearings in tibia, and bones of feet. Boring stitches in r. tibia during rest. Nocturnal cramps in calves and soles. Tearing in muscles of legs when sitting and standing. Stitches in r. calf. Swelling of instep. Swelling of metatarsal bones. Burning itching in toes, as if they had been frostbitten. R. toe inflames round nail and forms an abscess (produced. R. T. C.). [Flatulent colic, where the flatus is incarcerated; gnawing pains; darting pains; sensation of trembling in inner parts. Pains in zygoma; in cheeks; in under jaw. Affections of angles of eye, particularly the inner; glands about neck and lower jaw diseased ovaries, particularly r. one. Yellow scabs behind ear. Flat taste obstructed evacuation of bowels. Micturition too frequent; too sparing. Polypus; arthritic nodes; restlessness of the body; scurvy. From mental affections; anger; grief; mortification, esp. if caused by offence from loss of fluids; tobacco; Mercury; sexual excesses; sleeping in afternoon; touching the parts (as in toothache, can't bear to have the tongue, drink, or anything touch the teeth); from the least touch on affected parts. > After breakfast; from breaking wind. H. N. G.]. Has been used as an application for healing recent wounds. Paralytic drawing in joints, esp. during movement, or when the parts are in a false position. Drawing tearing in muscles, esp. when seated. Twitches at night. Acute, penetrating, deep lancinations in different parts. Cramps in limbs. Painful inflammations of bones; suppuration of bones and periosteum. Swelling of bones. Semilateral paralysis, after a fit of anger. Syncope. Painful sensibility of all muscles, when touched, and of joints, when using them. Mechanical injuries from sharp-cutting instruments. Painful weariness and excessive lassitude, esp. during movement; > by sitting or lying down. Continued disposition to remain lying down. Great fatigue, early in morning, with stiffness of all joints. Sore and stiff all over, swollen fingers and sore tibiæ (produced. R. T. C.). After a siesta, cloudiness, with heaviness in limbs. Relieves pains of cancer (R. T. C.).

Staphisagria. In the room vertigo, like stupefaction, not in the open air. On stooping and on turning the head quickly vertigo; every thing whirled round in half a circle (only once.). Vertigo : when walking he ran up against the door. Vertigo when lying in bed in the evening as if all turned round with him. Whirling vertigo, especially when sitting, diminished by walking about (aft. 1 h.). Giddy (aft. 8 1/2 h.). When standing and speaking confusion of the head, as if vertigo would come on, lasting a long time (aft. 14 h.). Whirling in the forehead and dulness in the head (aft. 5 h.). Confusion of the head, only in front in the centre of the forehead on a small spot the size of a finger-tip, like stupidity-in the street he did not know whether he was walking to the right or left; he had to take great care. Confusion of the head only in fits; sometimes his head was quite free and clear. The head is confused, as if stupid and heavy (aft. 1/2 h.). The head is always confused and the spirits depressed. Obtuseness of the mind, which kept him from work of all sorts. Dazed in the head as in catarrh. When he wishes to seize on an idea it escapes him. Vanishing of the thoughts; when he speaks or reflects on any subject and some one interrupts him, or suggests to him another thought, he immediately forgets the first thought and cannot recall it. (f-h1]. Vanishing of the thoughts (memory disturbed by fancies); when he reflects on anything so many things confusedly mixed together occur to him that he cannot get rid of them and quite forgets what he wished to think about. Weakness of memory : when he has read something after a few minutes he remembers it only dimly, and when he thought about anything for himself it soon after escaped him, and after long reflection he could hardly recall it. Pain in the whole head like tingling. (aft. 5 h.). Tingling and shooting in the whole head, worse when stooping forwards and walking, in the evening, for many hours (aft. 36 h.). In the morning quite dazed in the head with contractive pressure in the crown (aft. 4 d.). Headache in the crown like contraction from all sides and pressure. Headache, alternately stupefying and boring. Aching stupefying pain in the head, especially in the forehead, more severe when moving the head and when standing. A heavy weight seems to lie on the cribriform plate of the ethmoid bone, above the root of the nose, like a kneaded-together lump. Headache when moving as if all the brain would fall out; also when at rest as if the brain were pressed together, were separated from the skull and lay loosely in it. On stooping forwards pain in the head as if everything would come out at the forehead (aft. 5 h.). Headache as if the brain were compressed (chiefly in the forehead), with attacks of roaring in the ears, which goes off much sooner than the headache. He feels as if the occiput were compressed inwardly and outwardly. ({Hrr.]. A pressing of the brain, especially in the occiput, against the skull bones, and aching in it, as if too much blood had collected there, in the evening before going to bed, which continues after lying down (aft. 39 h.). {Hnl.]. A forcing-out and asunder-pressing pain in the left half of the forehead (aft. 1/2 h.). Aching asunder-pressing heavy pain in the occiput, when walking in the open air (aft. 1/2 h.). Heavy pressure over the right orbit, in the open air (aft. 3 1/2 h.). Heaviness in the head (aft. 72 h.). Heaviness of the head relieved by supporting it on the hand (aft. 1 h.). When he shook his head he felt on a small spot in the middle of the forehead as if there were something heavy there, like a leaden bullet in the brain, which could not be detached. Hard pressure in the head in the region of the right temporal bone and vertex. ({Hrr.]. Hard pressure on the right side of the forehead. Pressure above the right eye and drawing upwards. Pressure above the right eye behind the superciliary ridge, as from something hard. Pressive pain in the left temple outwardly and inwardly, as if the finger were strongly pressed on it (aft. 1 1/2 h.). Occasional drawing pressure in the forehead. In the morning immediately after waking, severe headache, as if the brain were torn, which went off afterwards with frequent spasmodic yawning. Tearing in the forehead, in the evening when sitting; on stooping there was shooting in it and it was relieved by walking. Violent tearing pressure through the left half of the brain, especially severe in the forehead, gradually increasing and gradually going off (aft. 54 h.). (f-h1]. Dull, painful, sometimes shooting pressure outwards, first in the whole forehead, then only in the left frontal protuberance, which went off during rest, but returned more violently during movement (aft. 4 h.). Occasional sharp pressure on the vertex. Shooting pain in the head, all day (aft. 17 d.). Aching stitch-like and drawing pain in the left side of the forehead (aft. 2h.). Dull pinching pain in the forehead, with stitches on the temples, which went off by walking, but returned when sitting and standing (aft. 4 h.). Quick stitches in the top of the frontal bone, making him start. Boring stitch from within outwards in the vertex (aft. 56 h.). Single coarse obtuse stitches from the skull into the brain, not far from the crown; at the same time the part is very painful externally, especially when touched. Pressive boring stitch, lasting a minute, in the whole left half of the forehead, from within outwards, the violence of which wakes him up twice from sleep in the morning (aft. 22 1/2 h.). Sharp burning needle-pricks in the left temple. Transient burning stitches in the occiput, the first days from the right to the left side, the following days from below upwards. Shooting in the left temple. Obtuse shooting in the right temple, outwardly and inwardly, as if the bone would be pressed out, more violent when touched. Drawing cutting tearing in the side of the forehead. Burning shooting pains on the left parietal bone. On the frontal bone externally, burning stitches. Itching over the hairy scalp. Eroding itching all over the occiput, which compels scratching, by which, however, it is rather aggravated than ameliorated (aft. 14 d.). On the upper part of the occiput, an eroding itching, with sore pain, which returns at the same time of the evening and in the same place. Itching erosion on the hairy scalp, increased by rubbing, for several days. Itching on the hairy scalp, like needle-pricks, and eruption of small pimples on the forepart towards the forehead. On the hairy scalp as also immediately above and behind the ear, an itching, scurfy eruption. The hairy scalp itches much, is scurfy and exudes watery discharge. Fine burning needle-pricks externally on the vertex. Much hair can be drawn from the head without pain, by slightly pulling it (aft. 4 h.). His hair falls out very much. Burning in-pressive pain in the left parietal bone, just above the ear (aft. 2 1/2h.). At the occiput from the occipital joint upwards, rheumatic aching drawing on bending the head forwards. Painful drawing externally on several parts of the head, more violent when touched. Tearing externally on the head and in the teeth.
